materialChanges: Retitled the question-and-claim section so its heading matches the distinction the prose actually teaches.
Defined turbidity in ordinary language at first use so the reduced-support pond task remains usable by a college-intro learner.
Made the ten-point, every-criterion mastery rule explicit and connected missed criteria to feedback, alternate cases, and unlimited retry.
Added a practice-scene pointer to the final recovery scaffold so learners can find the four-heading route when support fades.
Trimmed glossary scene links for inference, testable question, and hypothesis to scenes where the term or a listed alias is actually used.
Regenerated the deterministic PREM-SCI-001 reader artifact from the final canonical pack.

reviewDispositions.rationale: The exact original candidate already ends content/01-notice.md with the requested learner-visible source-note naming claim-observation-explanation, claim-empirical-question, claim-multiple-scientific-methods, and source-nrc-framework. Adding another block would duplicate correct evidence rather than repair a gap.

The final lesson preserves the accurate distinction: empirical questions are answerable, while hypotheses and claims are propositions that evidence can count against. Source support and assessment logic remain aligned.

The concrete apple opening, commitment before explanation, ordinary-language observation/explanation contrast, and human first-read voice were preserved because they already orient the declared learner clearly.
The reduced-support pond checklist now glosses turbidity as a measure of how cloudy the water is before the model question uses the term.
The heading now reads 'Empirical questions and falsifiable claims,' matching the section's explicit logical distinction without implying an unexplained third synonym.

The mastery-check section now states that mastery requires every rubric criterion and all ten points, and it directs a learner who misses a criterion to use feedback and retry with the alternate case.
The independent-practice section now points forward to the named Recovery route and explains that it provides the four-heading scaffold.

The complete worked model, fading support, retrieval-before-feedback sequence, misconception repair, novel clinic transfer, alternate retry cases, and delayed retrieval links were preserved and passed final repository verification.

Glossary navigation was tightened: inference now links only to scene-notice, hypothesis only to scene-build, and testable question no longer links to scene-notice; scenes using the term or an alias remain linked.

evidenceChecked: All four scenes retain one level-one heading, logical level-two structure, ordered source reading, and text-first prompts before feedback or model answers.
Every learning task can be completed in prose, bullets, or a table; none depends on color, image, audio, motion, hover, dragging, speed, physical apple preparation, or laboratory access.
The assessment is untimed and retakeable, exposes reasoning-rich feedback and alternate retry cases, and does not require a sensory, motor, or timed route.

The fictional clinic case is explicitly aggregated, contains no real patient data, requests no identity or personal medical details, and keeps the lesson educational rather than diagnostic or clinical.

Canonical scenes contain no raw HTML, scripts, remote embeds, executable notebooks, active SVG, unsafe TeX, external media, or third-party assets; repository validation and browser security/access-path tests passed.

The sole source record names its public terms route, a 2026-07-20 checked-no-agent-restriction-found status, facts-only original-expression use, exact locator, displayed copyright status, and no reused source expression, figure, table, example, or dataset.

The National Academies chapter and current legal-information page were rechecked through their public HTTPS routes; the cited chapter supports empirical questions, records and measurements, observation/explanation boundaries, non-causal interpretation, and plural scientific practices.

ATTRIBUTION.md declares CC BY 4.0 lesson content, facts-only source use, original fictional cases, absence of third-party assets or datasets, accountable authorship, and material author-agent provenance.

Strict source preflight passed for the one source record, and full verification passed schemas, claim/source-note coverage, rights contracts, deterministic rendering, protocol tests, keyboard/print paths, and narrow-viewport behavior.
limitations: No real learner testing was available; the first-read review and finalizer judgment are not evidence that students learned.
No manual screen-reader session was run specifically on this final lesson; equivalent source paths and repository-wide automated keyboard, print, and narrow-viewport checks were used.
Non-web PDF, EPUB, DOCX, and JATS exporters are not implemented, so parity was audited from canonical semantic source rather than generated exports.
The source-access and reuse audit applies the project policy and records a dated terms observation; it is not legal advice or a warranty from the source owner.

rationale: The two eligible advisory inputs are the unchanged academic and learning-design artifacts submitted as non-dismissed COMMENT reviews on GitHub PR #28 against original candidate 005c855a46fb1ad2603d58b622f9fd70d36f4bb5. They provide one academic and one learning-design input across distinct Google and Anthropic runs, satisfying standard-lesson-v3 with one approval and one change request. The finalizer read the original Git objects, preserved the strong human-first instructional arc, made the bounded clarifications recorded above, and disposed all nine indexed findings. The academic request for a missing scene-1 source note was not implemented because that exact source note already exists in the original candidate; its dissent is retained below. The final lesson remains aligned to the college-intro outcome, and the checked National Academies source supports the bounded claims within the recorded scope, although the academic review noted that it did not separately assess broader K-12 curriculum alignment. The final accessibility-and-rights audit is satisfied, strict source preflight passed, and npm run verify passed repository validation, all 55 protocol tests, and 22 browser tests with 12 container-only tests skipped. No credible academic, learning-design, accessibility, rights, privacy, security, or protocol blocker remains, so merge is the accountable decision for final commit 8f412e990e4ebdf0365d0e526a940ef19e4cb17e.
dissent: The academic reviewer requested changes because it reported that content/01-notice.md omitted a learner-visible source note. The exact original candidate contains that complete source note, so the finalizer records no change required and preserves this disagreement rather than duplicating the block.

The academic review declared that it did not independently review K-12 curriculum alignment beyond the cited NRC Framework. The finalizer checked the lesson against the repository's college-intro graph outcome and bounded the merge decision to this introductory scientific-reasoning scope.
conditions: The maintainer may make only the permitted publication transition after finalization: lesson status and source-confidence fields plus retained claims' review-status fields; any teaching-content change makes this adjudication stale.
